title: UNESCO-Madanjeet Singh Prize 2026: Win US$100,000 for Promoting Tolerance and Non-Violence Worldwide
summary: Nominations are now open for the 2026 UNESCO-Madanjeet Singh Prize for the Promotion of Tolerance and Non-Violence , one of the world’s most prestigious international awards recognizing outstanding efforts to advance peace, dialogue, and inclusion. Presented by UNESCO , the prize is awarded every two years and carries a US$100,000 award . It honours individuals, institutions, entities, and non-governmental organizations that have made exceptional contributions to promoting tolerance and non-violence through science, art, culture, and communication. The award celebrates initiatives that foster mutual understanding, respect for diversity, and peaceful coexistence across communities and nations. About the UNESCO-Madanjeet Singh Prize Established in 1995 on the initiative and generosity of UNESCO Goodwill Ambassador Madanjeet Singh, the prize recognizes achievements that align with UNESCO’s commitment to building peace through education, culture, science, and communication. The award seeks to highlight innovative and impactful efforts that address intolerance, discrimination, and violence while promoting dialogue and human dignity. Prize Award The laureate will receive: US$100,000 cash prize International recognition from UNESCO Global visibility for their work and impact Who Can Be Nominated Eligible nominees include: Individuals Institutions Organizations Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) Nominees must demonstrate significant leadership and measurable impact in promoting: Tolerance Non-violence Peacebuilding Dialogue Inclusion Respect for diversity Areas of Contribution Nominations are encouraged for work carried out in: Science Arts Culture Communication Education Community development Why This Prize Matters The UNESCO-Madanjeet Singh Prize highlights the importance of grassroots and institutional efforts to counter hatred, discrimination, and violence. It recognizes those whose work helps create more peaceful, just, and inclusive societies worldwide. Nomination Deadline All nominations must be submitted by: 19 June 2026 For additional information, contact: tolerance.prize@unesco.org How to Submit a Nomination Learn more about the eligibility criteria and nomination process here. For more Award Opportunities, Visit OFY here. The post UNESCO-Madanjeet Singh Prize 2026: Win US$100,000 for Promoting Tolerance and Non-Violence Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Flight PS752 Commemorative Scholarship Program 2026 in Canada: Funding for Post-Secondary Students Honouring the Victims of Flight PS752
summary: Applications are now open for the Flight PS752 Commemorative Scholarship Program 2026 , a meaningful scholarship initiative that honours the lives and legacies of the victims of Ukraine International Airlines Flight PS752. The program supports full-time post-secondary students studying in Canada whose academic work aligns with the educational and professional backgrounds of the victims or focuses on air disaster prevention and aviation safety. In 2025, the scholarship program awarded 36 scholarships to Canadian and international students from 19 educational institutions across six provinces , including doctoral, master’s, bachelor’s, and diploma students. This scholarship provides both financial support and an opportunity for students to contribute to fields of study that reflect the aspirations and accomplishments of those lost in the tragedy. About the Scholarship Program The Flight PS752 Commemorative Scholarship Program was established to preserve the memory of the victims by supporting students whose studies embody similar areas of academic and professional excellence. Eligible applicants must demonstrate how their current field of study connects to: The academic or professional backgrounds of the victims of Flight PS752, or Research and initiatives related to air disaster prevention and aviation safety Scholarship Benefits Selected recipients receive financial assistance to support their post-secondary education in Canada. In addition to funding, recipients become part of an important legacy initiative that recognizes academic excellence, innovation, and remembrance. Who Can Apply Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be enrolled as a full-time student Study at a Canadian post-secondary institution , including universities and community colleges Have completed at least one year of their current program Have at least one year remaining in their studies Demonstrate alignment between their field of study and the scholarship’s commemorative objectives Eligible Institutions Students from all recognized post-secondary institutions in Canada are eligible, including: Universities Community colleges Polytechnic institutions Both Canadian and international students studying in Canada may apply. Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by: 9 June 2026 How to Apply Learn more and apply here. For more Scholarship Opportunities, Visit OFY here. The post Flight PS752 Commemorative Scholarship Program 2026 in Canada: Funding for Post-Secondary Students Honouring the Victims of Flight PS752 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Landau Family Foundation Oakland Open Call 2026: Grants of $10,000–$25,000 for Arts, Housing, and Workers’ Rights Organizations
summary: Applications are now open for the Landau Family Foundation Oakland Open Call 2026 , a new grant opportunity supporting grassroots organizations and coalitions advancing social, racial, and economic justice in Oakland, California. The Landau Family Foundation is launching its first-ever open call in Oakland to fund organizations working in community arts, arts and activism, housing justice, and workers’ rights. The foundation prioritizes organizations rooted in and accountable to communities most impacted by racial, social, and economic injustice. About the Grant Opportunity This open call is designed to support local leaders, grassroots innovation, advocacy, and creative expression as drivers of systemic change. Funding can be used for: General operating support Program expenses Capacity-building initiatives The foundation seeks to reduce barriers to funding and invest in community-led solutions that promote long-term equity and justice. Grant Amount Selected organizations will receive grants ranging from: US$10,000 to US$25,000 Number of Awards 10 to 20 organizations and coalitions will be selected during this funding cycle. Priority Program Areas Eligible applicants may work in one or more of the following areas: Community arts Arts and activism Housing and housing justice Workers’ rights and labor justice Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be organizations or coalitions based in or serving Oakland, California Focus on social, racial, and economic justice Be rooted in and accountable to impacted communities Have an annual operating budget of US$5 million or less Application Process The first stage of the application is a Letter of Introduction (LOI) . Important details: LOIs are reviewed on a first come, first reviewed basis Only the first 100 LOIs received will be reviewed during the spring cycle Early submission is strongly encouraged Information Session A free informational webinar will be held on: 22 May 2026 Application Window Opens: 12 May 2026 Closes: 5 June 2026 How to Apply Access the applicant guide, funding priorities, and application instructions here. For more Grant Opportunities, Visit OFY Here. The post Landau Family Foundation Oakland Open Call 2026: Grants of $10,000–$25,000 for Arts, Housing, and Workers’ Rights Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Royal Society Short Industry Fellowships 2026: Funded UK Secondments for Researchers in Academia and Industry
summary: Applications are now open for the Royal Society Short Industry Fellowships 2026 , a prestigious funding opportunity designed to strengthen collaboration between academia and industry in the United Kingdom. Offered by the The Royal Society , this fellowship scheme supports talented researchers—particularly early-career scientists—who wish to undertake short-term secondments in either academic or industrial settings. The program enables researchers to gain practical experience, build partnerships, and accelerate innovation by applying scientific knowledge across sectors. About the Short Industry Fellowships The Royal Society Short Industry Fellowships aim to promote knowledge exchange between universities, research institutes, and industry partners. Fellows can spend time in a host organization to develop new collaborations, explore commercial applications, and broaden the impact of their research. Funding can cover the applicant’s salary and, where applicable, the salary of a postdoctoral researcher during the secondment period. Fellowship Benefits Selected applicants may receive funding to cover: Basic salary of the applicant during the fellowship Salary support for a postdoctoral researcher, where applicable Costs associated with undertaking the secondment Opportunities to establish academic-industry collaborations Professional development and networking benefits Eligible Research Areas Applications are welcomed from researchers working within the Royal Society’s remit in the natural sciences, including: Biological sciences Biomedical sciences Chemistry Engineering Mathematics Physics Related scientific disciplines Who Can Apply The fellowship is open to: Researchers in academia in the United Kingdom Researchers working in industry in the United Kingdom Early-career researchers and established scientists Applicants seeking to undertake a short secondment between academia and industry Geographic Eligibility Applicants must be based in the United Kingdom and affiliated with eligible academic or industrial institutions. Why Apply? This fellowship offers a valuable opportunity to: Build meaningful cross-sector collaborations Explore real-world applications of research Gain industry or academic experience Expand professional networks Enhance career development and research impact Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by: 28 May 2026 How to Apply Learn more and apply here For more Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY here. The post Royal Society Short Industry Fellowships 2026: Funded UK Secondments for Researchers in Academia and Industry appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: CDPR Young Policy Scholar Programme 2026 in Pakistan: Research Opportunity for Pre-University Students Interested in Economics and Public Policy
summary: Applications are now open for the CDPR Young Policy Scholar Programme 2026, an exciting pre-university research programme in Pakistan designed for ambitious students who want to develop strong research, analytical, and policy-writing skills before starting university. Organized by the Consortium for Development Policy Research (CDPR), the four-week programme offers students practical exposure to evidence-based policy research while helping them build competitive academic profiles for university admissions in Pakistan and abroad. The programme is especially suitable for students interested in economics, public policy, governance, and development studies. Participants will learn how to conduct policy research, analyze development issues, and produce a publishable policy brief under expert mentorship. For students looking to make productive use of their summer vacations while gaining valuable academic and professional skills, this programme presents an excellent opportunity. About the CDPR Young Policy Scholar Programme The Young Policy Scholar Programme is a structured research training initiative aimed at introducing pre-college students to the full lifecycle of policy research. Participants will move step-by-step through the research process, beginning with identifying a policy issue and eventually producing their own policy brief based on evidence and analysis. The programme combines: Research training Policy analysis Academic writing Mentorship Critical thinking Evidence-based learning Students will also gain insight into major economic and governance challenges facing Pakistan and learn how policy decisions impact development outcomes. Programme Duration The programme will run for four weeks: Start Date: 30 June 2026 End Date: 23 July 2026 This intensive summer programme is designed to maximize learning and hands-on engagement within a short period. What Participants Will Learn The programme is designed to equip students with practical academic and research skills that are highly valuable for university applications and future careers. Participants Will Learn How To: Identify important policy and development questions Conduct evidence-based policy research Analyze economic and governance challenges Develop research and analytical thinking skills Write structured policy briefs Understand research methodologies Present evidence clearly and effectively Engage with real-world public policy issues Why Students Should Apply The programme offers several major benefits for students preparing for university and future academic or professional careers. Key Benefits of the Programme 1. Build a Strong Academic Profile Students can strengthen their university applications with practical research experience and policy-writing credentials. 2. Gain Research Experience Early The programme introduces participants to university-level research processes before entering higher education. 3. Develop Writing and Analytical Skills Participants improve critical thinking, structured writing, and evidence-based reasoning skills. 4. Produce a Publishable Policy Brief One of the biggest highlights of the programme is the opportunity to produce a publishable policy brief, which can significantly strengthen a student’s portfolio. 5. Receive Expert Mentorship Students will learn directly from experienced professionals and researchers working in development and public policy. 6. Understand Pakistan’s Development Challenges Participants gain deeper insight into Pakistan’s economy, governance systems, and public policy environment. Who Should Apply? The programme is ideal for: Pre-college students High school students Students preparing for university applications Young learners interested in economics and development Students curious about governance and public policy Students seeking research credentials for academic advancement Eligibility and Ideal Candidate Profile CDPR encourages applications from ambitious students who are eager to learn and grow academically. Ideal applicants are students who: Are currently in school and preparing for university Want to study abroad or at leading Pakistani universities Are interested in economics, governance, development, or policymaking Want hands-on research experience Are motivated to improve their academic and professional profile Application Requirements Applicants must complete the online application form and submit the required supporting documents. Required Documents 1. Most Recent School Transcript Applicants must submit their latest academic transcript or school record. 2. Cover Letter (300 Words) The cover letter should answer the following questions: Why are you interested in this programme? What development or policy problem interests you most, and why? What do you hope to do with the skills you gain? The cover letter is an important component of the application and should demonstrate motivation, curiosity, and strong communication skills. Click here for more and to apply Important Dates Applicants should carefully note the programme timeline. Key Dates Applications Open: 1 May 2026 Applications Close: 15 June 2026 Decisions Announced: Mid-June 2026 Programme Duration: 30 June – 23 July 2026 Students are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute issues. Why This Opportunity Matters Research skills are becoming increasingly important for university admissions, scholarships, and future careers. The CDPR Young Policy Scholar Programme gives students an early introduction to academic research and evidence-based policymaking, helping them stand out in competitive academic environments. For students interested in solving real-world problems, understanding development challenges, and preparing for future leadership roles, this programme provides a valuable starting point. Final Thoughts The CDPR Young Policy Scholar Programme 2026 is an excellent summer opportunity for ambitious students in Pakistan who want to strengthen their academic profile while gaining practical research and policy-writing experience. With expert mentorship, hands-on learning, and the opportunity to produce a publishable policy brief, the programme equips students with skills that can benefit them throughout their academic and professional journey. Students interested in economics, public policy, development, and governance should strongly consider applying before the application deadline. For more global fellowship opportunities Click here The post CDPR Young Policy Scholar Programme 2026 in Pakistan: Research Opportunity for Pre-University Students Interested in Economics and Public Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Inclusive Policy Fellowship 2026 at Future Forum for Young Cambodian Professionals: Applications Open for New Cohort
summary: Applications are now officially open for the Inclusive Policy Fellowship 2026 at Future Forum, an exciting opportunity designed to empower young Cambodian professionals who are passionate about public policy, governance, social impact, and national development. The fellowship aims to identify and support emerging Cambodian leaders who are committed to contributing meaningful ideas and policy solutions to the country’s future. Through this programme, selected fellows will engage in policy-focused learning, research, collaboration, and leadership development over a fellowship period running from June 2026 to March 2027. Future Forum is seeking 20 Cambodian policy fellows for the new cohort, offering participants a valuable platform to strengthen their policy knowledge, analytical skills, professional networks, and public leadership potential. Young professionals interested in shaping Cambodia’s policy landscape are strongly encouraged to apply before the application deadline. About the Inclusive Policy Fellowship 2026 The Inclusive Policy Fellowship is a leadership and policy development programme created to nurture the next generation of Cambodian policy thinkers and changemakers. The fellowship focuses on supporting young professionals who are interested in addressing critical policy challenges affecting Cambodia. Fellows will have the opportunity to deepen their understanding of policymaking processes, participate in policy discussions, and contribute ideas that can positively influence inclusive national development. The programme is expected to create a collaborative learning environment where fellows can exchange perspectives, conduct research, and work alongside experts, practitioners, and fellow emerging leaders. Fellowship Duration The fellowship tenure will: Commence on 13 June 2026 Conclude at the end of March 2027 This extended fellowship period allows participants to engage meaningfully in learning activities, policy discussions, mentorship opportunities, and collaborative initiatives. Number of Fellowship Positions Available Future Forum will select: 20 Cambodian policy fellows The limited number of slots makes the fellowship competitive, and applicants are encouraged to prepare strong applications demonstrating their motivation, leadership potential, and policy interests. Objectives of the Fellowship The Inclusive Policy Fellowship seeks to: Develop policy analysis and leadership skills among young Cambodians Promote inclusive policymaking approaches Encourage youth participation in public policy discourse Support evidence-based policy engagement Strengthen Cambodia’s future policy leadership pipeline Create networks among emerging professionals and policy experts Foster innovative solutions to national development challenges Why Young Professionals Should Apply The fellowship presents a valuable opportunity for Cambodian youth seeking careers or engagement in: Public policy Governance Development work Advocacy Research Civic leadership Social innovation Public administration Community development Participants are likely to gain practical exposure to policy processes while building relationships with professionals and institutions involved in policymaking and development. Expected Benefits of the Fellowship Although the full programme details may be outlined by Future Forum, fellows can generally expect benefits such as: 1. Policy Training and Capacity Building Participants will strengthen their policy analysis, research, communication, and leadership skills. 2. Networking Opportunities Fellows will connect with policy experts, researchers, professionals, and fellow young leaders across Cambodia. 3. Mentorship and Guidance The programme is expected to provide mentorship opportunities from experienced practitioners and policy professionals. 4. Practical Policy Exposure Participants may engage in policy discussions, collaborative research, workshops, or public engagement activities. 5. Leadership Development The fellowship supports the growth of future Cambodian leaders capable of contributing to inclusive national development. Who Should Apply? Future Forum encourages applications from young Cambodian professionals who are passionate about policymaking and national development. Ideal candidates may include: Researchers Development professionals Civil society actors Young academics Community leaders Public sector professionals Social entrepreneurs Advocacy professionals Governance and policy enthusiasts Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the Inclusive Policy Fellowship 2026, applicants must meet the following conditions. Age Requirement Applicants must be: Between 22 and 35 years old Nationality Requirement Applicants must be: Cambodian nationals Residency Requirement Candidates must be: Based in Cambodia Professional Requirement Applicants should be: Young professionals with interest or experience in policy-related work Policy Focus Alignment Applicants must also align with one of the required policy focus areas identified by Future Forum. Candidates are encouraged to carefully review the official fellowship information to understand the thematic policy areas prioritized under the programme. Why This Fellowship Matters Youth participation in policymaking is increasingly important in shaping inclusive and sustainable societies. The Inclusive Policy Fellowship provides a rare opportunity for young Cambodians to contribute ideas, engage with policy conversations, and build the skills necessary to influence future development outcomes in Cambodia. By investing in emerging leaders, the programme contributes to strengthening democratic participation, civic engagement, and evidence-based policymaking in the country. Application Deadline Click here for more and to apply Interested applicants must submit their applications before: 11:00 AM on 22 May 2026 Late applications may not be considered, so candidates are advised to apply as early as possible. Application Tips for Interested Candidates Applicants can improve their chances by: Clearly explaining their policy interests Demonstrating leadership experience Showing commitment to inclusive development Highlighting community or professional impact Aligning their interests with the fellowship’s policy focus areas Submitting complete and well-prepared application materials Final Thoughts The Inclusive Policy Fellowship 2026 at Future Forum represents an outstanding opportunity for young Cambodian professionals seeking to build expertise in public policy, governance, and leadership. Through mentorship, collaboration, policy engagement, and professional development, fellows will gain valuable experience that can help shape both their careers and Cambodia’s future. Young professionals passionate about creating positive social and policy impact should strongly consider applying before the deadline. title: FIAS Fellowship Programme 2027/2028 in France: Fully Funded 10-Month Research Fellowship for International Scholars
summary: The French Institutes for Advanced Study (FIAS) Fellowship Programme 2027/2028 is now officially open for applications, offering an exceptional opportunity for outstanding international researchers and scholars to conduct innovative research projects in France. This prestigious fellowship programme provides 10-month residencies at seven renowned Institutes for Advanced Study (IAS) across France, including Aix-Marseille, Cergy, Loire Valley (Orléans-Tours), Lyon, Montpellier, Nantes, and Paris. The programme is designed to support high-level researchers in the Social Sciences and Humanities (SSH), as well as scholars working in interdisciplinary fields connected to SSH. Through this initiative, fellows will benefit from a stimulating academic environment, access to leading research institutions, and the freedom to pursue independent research in one of Europe’s strongest intellectual ecosystems. For the 2027/2028 academic year, a total of 29 fellowship positions are available. About the FIAS Fellowship Programme The French Institutes for Advanced Study Fellowship Programme is one of Europe’s leading international fellowship opportunities for researchers and scientists. FIAS aims to attract talented scholars from around the world and provide them with the ideal conditions to develop ambitious and innovative research projects. The fellowship emphasizes academic freedom, interdisciplinary collaboration, and intellectual exchange. Selected fellows will join multidisciplinary cohorts composed of scholars from diverse academic backgrounds and nationalities. Fellows will also benefit from close connections with local universities, research centers, and laboratories throughout France. The programme creates a unique environment where researchers can focus entirely on advancing their work without the administrative and teaching burdens that often accompany academic positions. Host Institutes Participating in the Programme The fellowship placements are distributed among seven French Institutes for Advanced Study. Available Positions for 2027/2028 Aix-Marseille: 7 positions Cergy: 3 positions Loire Valley (Orléans-Tours): 2 positions Lyon: 3 positions Montpellier: 2 positions Nantes: 4 positions Paris: 8 positions Each institute has its own scientific priorities, institutional partnerships, and research focus areas. Applicants are strongly encouraged to carefully review the webpages and academic profiles of the host institutes before applying to ensure alignment between their proposed research project and the institute’s strengths. Fields of Research Eligible for the Fellowship The FIAS Fellowship Programme welcomes applications from scholars working in: Social Sciences Humanities Interdisciplinary fields connected to Social Sciences and Humanities Examples of eligible disciplines may include: Political Science Sociology Anthropology Economics History Philosophy Literature Law International Relations Cultural Studies Human Geography Linguistics Psychology Education Studies Communication Studies Digital Humanities Environmental Humanities Science and Technology Studies Researchers whose work intersects with SSH disciplines are also encouraged to apply. Benefits of the FIAS Fellowship Programme The FIAS Fellowship Programme offers a highly supportive academic and professional environment for researchers seeking uninterrupted time to focus on their work. Key Benefits Include 1. Fully Supported Research Environment Fellows gain access to world-class academic institutions and research networks throughout France. 2. Academic Freedom Researchers are free to organize their work independently and pursue their own research priorities during the fellowship period. 3. Multidisciplinary Community Fellows become part of an international and interdisciplinary academic cohort, encouraging collaboration and intellectual exchange. 4. Access to Research Infrastructure Participants can collaborate with local laboratories, universities, and research centers associated with the host institutes. 5. International Networking Opportunities The programme provides opportunities to build long-term collaborations with leading scholars and institutions across Europe and beyond. 6. Prestigious Academic Recognition Being selected for a FIAS fellowship significantly enhances a researcher’s international academic profile and career prospects. Who Can Apply? The FIAS Fellowship Programme is open to outstanding researchers from around the world and at different career stages. Eligible Applicants Include Postdoctoral researchers Early-career researchers Mid-career academics Senior scientists Established scholars Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following eligibility requirements. Educational and Research Requirements Candidates must have: A PhD qualification At least two years of research experience after completing the PhD Important note: The PhD training period itself is not counted toward the required research experience. Residency Requirement Researchers from all countries are eligible, but applicants must not have spent more than 12 months in France during the three years prior to the application deadline. This requirement ensures the programme remains strongly international and promotes academic mobility. Scientific Selection Process The selection process for the FIAS Fellowship Programme is highly competitive and merit-based. Applications undergo an international independent peer-review evaluation process to ensure fairness and academic excellence. Evaluation Process Includes Eligibility screening Double peer-review assessment Review by the FIAS Selection Committee Final selection by the Scientific Advisory Boards of the Institutes for Advanced Study Selection is based primarily on: Academic excellence Quality and originality of the proposed research project Interdisciplinary relevance Scientific impact and innovation Alignment with host institute priorities Application Timeline Applicants should carefully note the official timeline for the 2027/2028 fellowship cycle. Important Dates Application Deadline: 25 June 2026 at 6:00 PM (Paris time) July 2026: Eligibility Check August – November 2026: Double Peer Review Process December 2026: Pre-selection by FIAS Selection Committee January – February 2027: Selection by IAS Scientific Advisory Boards March 2027: Announcement of Results How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Applicants are advised to: Download and carefully read the official call for applications Review the applicant guide thoroughly Research the host institutes before selecting preferences Prepare research proposals carefully and strategically Why Researchers Should Apply The FIAS Fellowship Programme stands out as one of the most prestigious international research opportunities in Europe. The programme offers scholars the rare opportunity to dedicate significant uninterrupted time to high-level research while engaging with a dynamic international academic community in France. Researchers seeking intellectual freedom, interdisciplinary collaboration, and global academic exposure will find this fellowship particularly valuable. For scholars aiming to strengthen their international research profile and contribute to innovative academic work, the FIAS Fellowship Programme 2027/2028 represents an exceptional opportunity. For more global fellowship opportunities Click here The post FIAS Fellowship Programme 2027/2028 in France: Fully Funded 10-Month Research Fellowship for International Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Sisters in Public Health Scholarship 2026–2027 in the USA | Fully Detailed Guide for Public Health Students
summary: The Sisters in Public Health (SiPH) Scholarship Program 2026–2027 is now accepting applications from women pursuing studies in public health across the United States. This annual scholarship initiative supports students at the Bachelor’s, Master’s, and Doctorate levels through three distinct scholarship opportunities worth $1,000 each. If you are a public health student seeking financial support, professional recognition, and access to a growing network of women leaders in healthcare and public health, this opportunity could be an excellent fit. Applications are officially open and will close on June 21, 2026, at 11:59 PM PST . About the Sisters in Public Health Scholarship Sisters in Public Health (SiPH) is committed to empowering women in public health through mentorship, networking, education, and scholarship opportunities. Every year, the organization awards scholarships to outstanding students who demonstrate academic achievement and dedication to advancing public health. For the 2026–2027 cycle, SiPH will award three scholarships: Bourda Scholarship – For Bachelor’s degree students Frazier Fund – For Master’s degree students Lockett Award – For Doctorate degree students Each scholarship recipient will receive $1,000 . Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants will receive: A $1,000 scholarship award Recognition from Sisters in Public Health Increased visibility within the public health community Networking opportunities with professionals and scholars Support toward academic and professional goals Available Scholarships and Eligibility Criteria 1. Bourda Scholarship (Bachelor’s Level) Applicants must: Be enrolled in a Bachelor’s program in Public Health Have completed at least the first year of the program Hold basic membership status with SiPH Be a full-time student Identify as a woman in public health Attend a school in the United States Have a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0 2. Frazier Fund (Master’s Level) Applicants must: Be enrolled in a Master’s program in Public Health Have completed at least one academic term Hold basic membership status with SiPH Be a part-time or full-time student Identify as a woman in public health Attend a school in the United States Have a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0 3. Lockett Award (Doctorate Level) Applicants must: Be enrolled in a Doctorate program in Public Health Have completed at least one academic term Hold basic membership status with SiPH Be a part-time or full-time student Identify as a woman in public health Attend a school in the United States Have a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0 Important Dates and Timeline Applicants should carefully note the following dates for the 2026 scholarship cycle: March 2, 2026: Scholarship applications open June 21, 2026: Application deadline (11:59 PM PST) July 20, 2026: Scholarship winners notified July 27, 2026: Acceptance deadline for winners August 3–7, 2026: Scholarship awards announced and distributed How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official scholarship application through the Sisters in Public Health website. Applicants are advised to review the Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) before submitting their applications. Please note: A Google account is required to complete the application form. Applicants should ensure all eligibility requirements are met before applying. Official Application Link To apply and learn more about the scholarship, visit the official website: APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here . Contact Information For scholarship-related questions, applicants may contact: Email: scholarship@sistersinpublichealth.org VP of Scholarship: Trisha Amboree Final Thoughts The Sisters in Public Health Scholarship Program is an excellent opportunity for women pursuing careers in public health to receive financial support and professional recognition. Whether you are completing your undergraduate studies or advancing through graduate or doctoral education, these scholarships can help ease financial burdens while connecting you with a supportive public health community. Students interested in applying should begin preparing their applications early and ensure submission before the June 21, 2026 deadline. Here are ready-to-use social media posts for LinkedIn, Instagram, and Facebook. title: Climate Leadership for Community Action Course 2026: Fully Online Climate Training for Young Leaders
summary: As climate challenges continue to affect communities worldwide, young people are increasingly stepping forward to drive innovative local solutions. The Climate Leadership for Community Action Course 2026 by DOT (Digital Opportunity Trust) is a unique opportunity for aspiring climate leaders to gain practical skills, mentorship, and global connections through a fully online learning experience. This six-week virtual program is designed to empower young changemakers with the tools, knowledge, and confidence needed to create meaningful climate action projects within their communities. Participants will join a cohort-based learning experience alongside peers from Africa and the Middle East while receiving mentorship from experienced DOT Climate Ambassadors. The program is ideal for individuals passionate about climate justice, sustainability, advocacy, and community resilience — even if they have no prior experience in climate action. Why You Should Apply for the Climate Leadership for Community Action Course The Climate Leadership for Community Action Course goes beyond traditional online learning. It combines practical climate education, participatory action research, leadership development, and peer collaboration to help participants turn ideas into real community solutions. Participants will: Learn directly from climate mentors and ambassadors Join a global network of youth climate leaders Gain practical leadership and advocacy skills Develop solutions tailored to local climate challenges Receive a certificate upon successful completion Get opportunities to pitch climate ideas for future innovation bootcamps This course is especially valuable for young people who want to create impact but may not have access to formal climate education or professional climate networks. Program Details Course Duration 6 weeks Approximately 5 hours per week 30 total learning hours Program Format 100% online Cohort-based learning model Live coaching sessions facilitated by DOT Climate Ambassadors Start Date May 11, 2026 Certificate Participants who successfully complete the course will receive a certificate by July 7, 2026. Application Deadline Register for Cohort 1 by May 26, 2026 What Participants Will Learn The course curriculum is designed to provide practical climate leadership skills and community-centered knowledge. Climate Science: Participants will explore the fundamentals of climate systems, environmental impacts, and global climate change. Community-Led Climate Solutions: Learners will understand how to design and implement local climate initiatives using Participatory Action Research (PAR) methods. Climate Justice: The course examines the social and economic dimensions of climate change and how vulnerable communities are affected. Sustainable Development: Participants will gain insights into building resilient and sustainable communities. Climate Advocacy: The program teaches communication and advocacy skills for engaging policymakers, local communities, and online audiences. Participatory Approaches: Learners will develop facilitation and community engagement techniques for climate workshops and local initiatives. Leadership Development: The course supports young people in building confidence, leadership capacity, and public voice as climate champions. Who Should Apply? The Climate Leadership for Community Action Course is open to all interested learners. However, DOT especially encourages applications from: Young people aged 18–35 Applicants from Tanzania, Kenya, Ethiopia, Rwanda, Lebanon, Jordan, Malawi, and Zambia Individuals from climate-vulnerable communities Youth with limited access to climate education opportunities No prior experience is required to participate. What Makes This Program Unique? Learn Alongside Global Peers: Participants become part of a diverse learning community with youth leaders from Africa and the Middle East. Mentorship and Coaching: DOT Climate Ambassadors provide mentorship, support, and practical guidance throughout the program. Real-World Application: Participants are encouraged to apply their learning directly to climate challenges affecting their own communities. Future Innovation Opportunities: Selected participants may have the opportunity to pitch climate ideas and join future Climate Innovation Bootcamps. How to Apply Applicants are encouraged to apply early as spots are limited due to the cohort-based mentorship structure. For inquiries: Email: climate@dotrust.org Interested applicants can register online using the official registration link below: APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more short courses opportunities, click here . The post Climate Leadership for Community Action Course 2026: Fully Online Climate Training for Young Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Multimedia Consultant at United Nations Volunteers (UNV) – Home-Based Consultancy Opportunity for Creative Professionals
summary: The United Nations Volunteers is inviting applications for a Multimedia Consultant position to support the International Volunteer Year (IVY) Secretariat. This fully remote consultancy offers creative professionals the opportunity to contribute to global volunteerism campaigns through impactful digital storytelling, branding, and multimedia production. The consultancy is ideal for individuals with strong skills in video editing, graphic design, branding, and multimedia content creation who are passionate about using creativity to amplify international development and volunteer initiatives. Applicants selected for this consultancy will work closely with the IVY Secretariat to produce engaging visual content for digital platforms and outreach campaigns that strengthen the visibility and impact of UNV’s global communications efforts. About the Multimedia Consultant Role The Multimedia Consultant will support the production of high-quality graphics, videos, outreach materials, and branded digital content for the International Volunteer Year Secretariat. The consultant will contribute to the development of compelling multimedia materials that align with UNV branding and communication strategies. This consultancy is completely home-based, allowing qualified professionals from around the world to participate remotely. Key Responsibilities The selected consultant will be responsible for: Editing videos from already filmed IVY activities Producing branding products for regional and headquarters IVY teams Supporting multimedia production for digital platforms Designing graphics and outreach materials Developing engaging visual communication assets Assisting with branded campaign materials and digital storytelling Consultancy Duration The consultancy will run for: 90 working days Over approximately 6 months This flexible arrangement makes it suitable for experienced freelance multimedia professionals and consultants seeking international remote opportunities. Benefits of the Opportunity This consultancy provides several professional advantages, including: Opportunity to work with the United Nations system Fully remote and flexible work arrangement International exposure in multimedia communications Contribution to global volunteerism initiatives Portfolio-building experience with a globally recognized organization Networking opportunities with international development professionals Application Deadline The deadline to apply for this consultancy opportunity is: 21 May 2026 at 12:00 PM (New York time) Late applications may not be considered, so interested applicants are encouraged to submit their proposals early. How to Apply Applicants must submit their proposals directly through the official UNDP supplier portal. Before submitting, candidates are encouraged to create a draft response in the system to receive notifications about any amendments or updates to the tender requirements.
